summary.intent: Audit all eight date-time agnostic components for Epic #224 closure, verifying spec completeness, then split the monolithic category-level spec-conformance and proptest test files into per-component modules to match the pattern already established by data-display and navigation categories.
summary.outcome: All audit findings were fixed (spec category file updated from 6 to 8 components, stale test-path references patched in two component specs, stale ars-core path comments fixed in date-field.md), both test files were split into per-component directory modules, branch coverage was brought above the 90% threshold with targeted tests, and the full fast CI gate passed before commit and push.

repo: The pre-push gate is `cargo xci --profile fast`; it includes formatting, clippy, unit/nextest, spec validation, and a final native LLVM branch-coverage pass that must clear per-package thresholds.
`cargo xcov` runs only the coverage step and is much faster than the full gate — useful for iterating on coverage gaps before spending another full 10-15 min `xci-fast` pass.

The exact ars-components branch coverage threshold is 90% with numerator/denominator tracked in `target/coverage/native-fast.lcov`; `cargo xtask coverage check --file target/coverage/native-fast.lcov --package ars-components --min 90 --branch-min 90` prints the exact fraction.

Per-component test modules follow the data-display/navigation precedent: `tests/spec_conformance/<category>/<component>.rs` + `mod.rs`, and `tests/proptest_state_machines/<category>/<component>.rs` + `mod.rs` with a shared `helpers.rs`.

Splitting a proptest category file requires changing `#![proptest_config(super::common::proptest_config())]` to `#![proptest_config(crate::common::proptest_config())]` in each per-component child module.
The spec category overview file `_category.md` is not automatically kept in sync with Epic scope; it must be manually updated when new components are added to the category.
`cargo xtask spec info <component>` and `cargo xtask spec validate` are the canonical tools for checking spec registration and manifest correctness.
Running multiple `cargo xtask spec info` calls in parallel causes Cargo package-cache lock contention and serializes them anyway.

code.finding: Category overview still described 6 components and omitted RangeCalendar and DateTimePicker even though Epic #224 and the manifest both included 8. Must be kept in sync manually.
1,276-line monolithic category blob; was split into date_time/{helpers,mod,calendar,date_field,date_picker,date_range_field,date_range_picker,date_time_picker,range_calendar,time_field}.rs.
sync_sub_value time-segment path and TypeBufferCommit readonly guard were uncovered branches that kept ars-components below the 90% branch threshold.
Testing appendix still referenced the old category blob path tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs instead of the new per-component module.
Same stale category blob path reference as date-range-field.md.
Code-example comments used ars-core/src/components/... paths; corrected to crates/ars-components/src/date_time/...

workflow: Using a Python script piped through `exec_command` to parse LCOV files and rank missed branch lines by frequency is the fastest way to target coverage gaps without waiting for annotated HTML reports.

When splitting a large test file using line-range slices, always regenerate from `git show HEAD:<path>` (canonical source) rather than the working-tree file after partial edits to avoid off-by-one errors from already-applied changes.
The `nl -ba` command is useful for verifying exact line boundaries before committing to slice operations on large files.

Coverage branch threshold failures near the minimum require multiple small targeted tests and repeated `cargo xcov` runs; each full pass takes several minutes so targeting branches with confirmed miss records in the LCOV data is essential to avoid wasted cycles.

Temp split files (.tmp) should be cleaned up immediately with `rm` before switching the harness module declaration, to avoid the compiler seeing both the old file and the new directory simultaneously.

friction: The 90% branch coverage threshold was extremely tight (9514/10572 branches covered); four separate `cargo xcov` iterations were needed, each taking several minutes, before the threshold cleared.

Several targeted tests (typeahead time segment, sync_sub_value path) compiled and passed but did not register as new covered branches in the LCOV data, likely due to instrumentation deduplication or inlining — required reading raw BRDA records to find branches that would actually count.

The initial Python-based line-range split used incorrect slice boundaries, causing extra closing braces and missing struct derives to appear in the generated files; required re-reading from `git show HEAD:` and regenerating.
Multiple parallel `cargo xtask spec info` calls serialized on the Cargo lock, providing no speedup over sequential calls.
Each full `cargo xci --profile fast` pass takes 10-15 minutes; having to rerun it twice (once after finding the coverage failure, once after fixing it) was the largest time cost in the session.
open_items: Epic #224 was not closed on GitHub — the user needs to do that manually now that the PR is pushed.
The PR creation step (`gh pr create`) is shown as the last action in the transcript but its completion output is not shown — verify the PR was successfully opened.

Adapter specs (Leptos/Dioxus) for the date-time category were confirmed to exist and reference all 8 components, but were not audited for content drift against the agnostic spec — that is adapter-layer work and was explicitly out of scope for this epic.
The `ars-components` branch coverage is now just above 90% — any future branch additions to date_time_picker or calendar/grid.rs risk dropping it below threshold again; the margin is thin.
